questions.q: What was Vishvamitra before he became a sage?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra was originally a king who later attained the status of a brahmarshi through severe penance.

questions.q: Whom did Vishvamitra take with him to guard his sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra asked King Dasharatha for the young princes Rama and Lakshmana to protect his sacrifice.

questions.q: What did Vishvamitra teach Rama and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra imparted knowledge of divine astras (celestial weapons) to Rama and Lakshmana.

questions.q: Which king's court did Vishvamitra visit to request Rama's help?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra came to King Dasharatha's court in Ayodhya to request that Rama protect his sacrifice.

questions.q: What sacred title did Vishvamitra ultimately attain through penance?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra achieved the exalted rank of brahmarshi, the highest order of sages.

questions.q: In which kingdom did King Dasharatha rule when Vishvamitra arrived?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha ruled the kingdom of Ayodhya, where Vishvamitra came seeking the princes' aid.

questions.q: Vishvamitra is often described as which kind of figure?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra is known as the warrior-sage, combining his royal martial heritage with spiritual attainment.

questions.q: What was being threatened that prompted Vishvamitra to seek Rama's protection?
questions.explanation: Demons were disrupting Vishvamitra's yajna, so he sought Rama to guard the sacrifice.

questions.q: Who was the great sage and family priest of Ayodhya that Vishvamitra's rivalry is famously associated with?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra's long rivalry was with the sage Vasishtha, the royal priest of the Ikshvaku dynasty.

questions.q: Rama and Lakshmana were of what age group when Vishvamitra took them?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana describes Rama and Lakshmana as young princes when Vishvamitra led them away.

questions.q: Which demoness did Rama slay near the start of his journey with Vishvamitra?
questions.explanation: At Vishvamitra's urging, Rama killed the demoness Tataka who terrorized the forest region.

questions.q: Which demon, son of Tataka, did Rama hurl far across the sea during the protection of the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Rama struck Maricha with the Manava astra and flung him far across the ocean, sparing his life.

questions.q: Which demon was killed outright by Rama while defending Vishvamitra's sacrifice?
questions.explanation: While Maricha was flung away, his companion Subahu was slain by Rama during the yajna's defense.

questions.q: Whose hermitage, associated with Vamana, did Vishvamitra establish his sacrifice near?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra's sacrifice took place at Siddhashrama, the hermitage linked to Vishnu's Vamana avatar.

questions.q: To which city did Vishvamitra lead Rama and Lakshmana after the sacrifice was completed?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra took the princes to Mithila, King Janaka's capital, where the great bow was kept.

questions.q: At Mithila, which feat did Vishvamitra encourage Rama to attempt?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra prompted Rama to lift the mighty bow of Shiva, which Rama broke, winning Sita's hand.

questions.q: Whom did Rama restore from stone to her human form on the way to Mithila, guided by Vishvamitra?
questions.explanation: On the journey, Rama's touch released Ahalya, wife of sage Gautama, from her curse.

questions.q: The two divine weapons 'bala' and 'atibala' that Vishvamitra gave the princes protected them from what?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra taught Rama and Lakshmana the bala and atibala mantras to ward off hunger, thirst, and weariness.

questions.q: What was Vishvamitra's royal name or dynasty before renouncing kingship?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra was born in the Kushika line and is often called Kaushika after his ancestor Kushika.

questions.q: Initially Dasharatha was reluctant to send Rama. Who persuaded him to honor Vishvamitra's request?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha counseled Dasharatha to send Rama, assuring him of Vishvamitra's greatness and Rama's safety.

questions.q: What kind of weapons did Vishvamitra bestow upon Rama, drawn from divine sources?
options: A wide array of astras and their recall mantras
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra granted Rama numerous celestial astras along with the mantras to invoke and withdraw them.

questions.q: Vishvamitra's penance was once disturbed by a celestial nymph sent to test him. Who was she?
questions.explanation: The apsara Menaka was sent to disrupt Vishvamitra's austerities, a famous episode in his legend.

questions.q: Vishvamitra famously attempted to send which king bodily to heaven, creating a parallel heaven in the sky?
questions.explanation: When Vasishtha's sons refused, Vishvamitra used his powers to send King Trishanku skyward and began creating a new heaven.

questions.q: The conflict that first turned Vishvamitra toward asceticism began over which possession of Vasishtha?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra coveted Vasishtha's divine cow Kamadhenu (Nandini), and his failure to seize her by force drove him to penance.

questions.q: Which rank did Vishvamitra hold before brahmarshi, which he found unsatisfying?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra progressed from rajarshi (royal sage) to maharshi before finally being recognized as a brahmarshi.

questions.q: Who finally addressed Vishvamitra as 'brahmarshi', acknowledging his supreme attainment?
questions.explanation: It was his old rival Vasishtha who at last honored Vishvamitra with the title brahmarshi, ending their feud.

questions.q: The sacred Gayatri mantra is traditionally attributed as a revelation to which sage?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Vishvamitra as the rishi who revealed the Gayatri mantra in the Rig Veda.

questions.q: Which devastating weapon of Vasishtha repeatedly devoured Vishvamitra's astras, teaching him brahminical power surpasses kshatriya might?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha's brahmadanda absorbed every celestial weapon Vishvamitra launched, convincing him to seek brahmin spiritual power.

questions.q: In the Bala Kanda, the recounting of the descent of the Ganga (Ganga-avatarana) is narrated to Rama by which sage?
questions.explanation: During the journey, Vishvamitra narrates the story of Ganga's descent and other legends to Rama and Lakshmana.

questions.q: Vishvamitra cursed the sons of Vasishtha to be born as outcastes after they did what?
options: Refused to help and ridiculed King Trishanku
questions.explanation: When Vasishtha's sons scorned Trishanku and refused to officiate, an enraged Vishvamitra cursed them to fall into degradation.
